intended_use: Axial3D Insight is intended for use as a cloud-based service and image segmentation framework for the transfer of DICOM imaging information from a medical scanner to an output file. The Axial3D Insigh
intended_use_full: Axial3D Insight is intended for use as a cloud-based service and image segmentation framework for the transfer of DICOM imaging information from a medical scanner to an output file. The Axial3D Insight output file can be used for the fabrication of physical replicas of the output file using additive manufacturing methods. The output file or physical replica can be used for treatment planning. The output file or physical replica can be used for diagnostic purposes in the field of orthopedic trauma, orthopedic, maxillofacial, and cardiovascular applications. Axial3D Insight should be used with other diagnostic tools and expert clinical judgment.
target_condition: Orthopedic trauma, orthopedic surgery, maxillofacial surgery, and cardiovascular conditions
ground_truth: RADPEER (peer reviewed medical imaging review framework) assessment by radiologists for segmentation performance; clinical expert physician review for intended use validation
other_metrics: Clinical Segmentation Performance study: 3 radiologists reviewed 12 cases across orthopedics, trauma, maxillofacial, and cardiovascular; all cases scored within acceptance criteria of 1 or 2a on RADPEER framework. Intended Use validation study: 9 physicians reviewed 12 cases across Orthopedics, Trauma, Maxillofacial, and Cardiovascular with successful validation of device outputs satisfying end user needs.
